framework_used: Jobs to Be Done (JTBD) + RICE Scoring
action_items: Add persona-branching onboarding wizard: ask 'What describes you best?' on first run and pre-configure approval gate defaults (P1=strict, P2=minimal, P3=tenant-scoped)
Build a compliance-first starter template for P1 that ships with IEC 62304 / ISO 13485 task YAML pre-wired and DHF document generation prompts
Create a zero-friction quickstart path for P2: single 'make demo' command that spins up a working 3-agent team from a plain-English description in under 5 minutes
Add per-tenant LLM spend metering and budget caps (cost_limit_usd per solution) to satisfy P3 FinOps requirements
Instrument audit_log.db with persona-segment tags so SAGE can surface per-archetype analytics and validate RICE assumptions empirically
Publish official SAGE Docker image with SSO/OIDC middleware hooks to unblock P3 enterprise procurement
Write a 'regulated industry' cookbook (SAGE + IEC 62304 traceability) as a publicly available reference implementation to drive P1 inbound

personas.name: Marcus Chen
personas.title: Engineering Manager, Medical Devices
personas.tagline: I need my AI agents to be auditable — my regulator will ask.

context.industry: Medical devices, avionics, automotive, railway, industrial safety
context.ai_maturity: Cautious adopter — has tried GitHub Copilot, wary of hallucinations in safety-critical paths

functional_job.statement: When managing a cross-functional engineering team on a regulated product, I want to automate the tedious compliance documentation, review cycles, and change impact analysis so that I can ship features faster without accumulating audit debt.
sub_jobs: Trace every AI-suggested code change to a requirement ID automatically
Generate draft DHF/IQ/OQ/PQ documentation from agent outputs
Route agent proposals through the correct approval chain based on risk tier
Keep a tamper-evident log of every AI decision for regulator inspection
Run DRC/ERC and static analysis on hardware artifacts without manual toolchain setup
functional_job.outcome_metric: Reduce compliance documentation effort by ≥40% per release cycle

emotional_job.statement: I want to feel confident that deploying AI in my workflow will not get me, my team, or my company sanctioned — and that I am not the last person standing between a bad AI output and a patient.
emotional_job.core_fear: An AI agent produces a plausible-sounding but wrong recommendation that passes review and causes a field failure or FDA warning letter
emotional_job.desired_feeling: In control — every agent action is visible, reversible, and traceable to a human decision
emotional_job.tension: Wants AI productivity gains but is professionally and personally liable for every output that ships

social_job.statement: I want to be known internally as the leader who modernised our development process without cutting corners on quality — and externally as someone regulators and customers trust to run a rigorous shop.
social_job.peer_signal: Peers at conferences who adopted AI tools recklessly got burned; this persona wants to be the counter-example
social_job.internal_narrative: 'I brought AI to our team AND we passed our ISO audit cleanly'
social_job.external_narrative: Presents SAGE-generated audit trail at notified body review as evidence of controlled AI use

trigger_events: Upcoming regulatory submission deadline with backlog of undocumented AI-assisted changes
New hire onboarding: needs agent-guided knowledge transfer for regulated processes
Post-incident: a manual review missed something; wants AI-assisted triage with human gate
Competitor shipped a comparable product 30% faster — pressure to accelerate without cutting compliance
FDA/CE MDR audit found traceability gaps in AI-assisted development
hiring_criteria.switch_threshold: Will evaluate SAGE when: (1) at least 1 compliance gap is attributed to manual documentation, OR (2) release velocity is below 60% of target for 2+ consecutive sprints due to documentation overhead
must_have_before_hiring: HITL approval gate is non-negotiable — agents must never self-approve safety-critical changes
Audit log is immutable and per-solution isolated
Graceful degradation if LLM provider is unavailable — no production stoppage
On-premise or private cloud deployment option for data residency requirements

dealbreakers: Any agent proposal executes without explicit human approval on safety-classified tasks
Audit log is incomplete, purgeable, or shared across solutions (cross-contamination risk)
Hallucinated compliance references (e.g., wrong standard version cited in draft DHF)
Cannot demonstrate to auditor that AI output was reviewed before implementation
Vendor lock-in to a single LLM provider violating procurement or security policy
firing_criteria.tolerance_level: Zero tolerance for compliance failures; moderate tolerance for capability gaps if roadmap is credible

current_solutions_displaced.use_case: Code suggestions for embedded C and firmware
current_solutions_displaced.why_insufficient: No audit trail, no domain runners for cross-compilation or DRC, no HITL gate for regulated changes

current_solutions_displaced.use_case: Manual requirements traceability and compliance documentation
current_solutions_displaced.why_insufficient: Fully manual, no AI generation, documentation debt accumulates faster than it is cleared
Domain-specific, no agent orchestration, no connection to software development lifecycle
No agent assistance for layout decisions, no integration with firmware or compliance workflows
Brittle, undocumented, not maintained, no LLM reasoning

tam.description: Global engineering leads in regulated industries (medical, aero/defense, auto, industrial)
tam.source: IDC 2024 Worldwide Safety-Critical Engineering Workforce Report; BLS SOC codes 17-2112, 17-2141, 17-2072

sam.description: Regulated engineering leads at companies with 50+ employees with active AI tooling budget (15% of TAM)
sam.rationale: IDC estimates 15% of regulated engineering orgs have approved AI-assisted development spend in 2025

som_3yr.description: Reachable via direct outreach, conference presence (MD&M, Embedded World, AutoSens), and open-source community — 3% SAM capture
som_3yr.growth_driver: FDA AI/ML SaMD guidance 2024 creating urgency for auditable AI tooling

willingness_to_pay_monthly_usd.rationale: Benchmarked against Jira Advanced Roadmaps ($35/user/mo) + Confluence ($10/user/mo) + compliance tooling — SAGE replaces $100–200/user/mo in tooling across a 5-person team

rice.note: High RICE due to strong willingness-to-pay and clear pain; effort moderate because runner and audit infrastructure already built

success_metrics: Compliance documentation effort reduced by ≥40% per release (hours saved per release cycle tracked in audit log)
Zero compliance findings attributable to AI-assisted changes in annual regulatory audit
HITL approval turnaround time under 4 hours for standard proposals (tracked in proposal_store.py timestamps)
Agent-generated DHF sections accepted by regulatory specialist with fewer than 2 revision rounds on average
Domain runner verification pass rate (DRC/ERC/static analysis) above 90% on first submission

pain_points.pain: Documentation debt compounds faster than the team can clear it — every AI-assisted change requires manual traceability updates in Confluence and Jira, consuming 15–25% of sprint capacity
pain_points.severity: Critical — directly blocks regulatory submissions and causes schedule slippage

pain_points.pain: No safe AI tooling exists for regulated domains — available tools (Copilot, ChatGPT) have no audit trail, no HITL gate, and no domain awareness for embedded C, PCB, or firmware verification
pain_points.severity: High — forces teams to ban AI tools outright or use them unofficially, creating shadow risk

pain_points.pain: Cross-functional context loss — when an engineer leaves or a consultant finishes, domain knowledge (design decisions, compliance rationale) evaporates; no institutional memory layer exists
pain_points.severity: Medium-High — onboarding new engineers costs 4–8 weeks; repeated mistakes in compliance reviews

personas.name: Priya Nair
personas.title: Solo Technical Founder
personas.tagline: I am the entire product, engineering, and ops department. I need agents that can be the rest.
representative_roles: Technical Co-Founder (pre-Series A, up to 5 employees)
Indie Hacker building a productised service

context.industry: Consumer apps, B2B SaaS, developer tools, content platforms, consulting
context.compliance_burden: Minimal to none — GDPR basics, App Store guidelines
context.ai_maturity: Power user — living in AI tools daily, frustrated by context-switching between ChatGPT, Cursor agents, and manual deployment

functional_job.statement: When building and iterating a product with a team of one, I want to delegate entire work streams — development, documentation, UX review, GTM copy — to autonomous agents so that I can focus only on decisions that require my judgment as the founder.
sub_jobs: Spin up a domain-specific agent team from a plain-English description in under 10 minutes
Have agents propose and self-execute routine tasks (CI fixes, dependency updates, README drafts) without interrupting my flow
Compound agent knowledge over time so I am not re-explaining the same context every session
Switch between LLM providers based on cost and capability without changing workflows
Build custom MCP tools that integrate my SaaS stack (Stripe, Supabase, Vercel) into agent context
functional_job.outcome_metric: Ship features 3x faster; reduce context-switching overhead to under 30 minutes per day

emotional_job.statement: I want to feel like I have a full team backing me — that I am not the bottleneck on every decision and that momentum compounds rather than resets every Monday morning.
emotional_job.core_fear: Running out of runway because I am too slow to ship, or burning out from being the single point of failure on everything
emotional_job.desired_feeling: Leverage — the sensation that the output of the week is disproportionate to the hours I put in
emotional_job.tension: Wants maximum autonomy from agents but does not want to lose control of product direction or quality bar

social_job.statement: I want to be known in founder and indie hacker communities as someone who figured out how to run a real company alone — as proof that the one-person billion-dollar company is possible.
social_job.peer_signal: Twitter/X threads from founders showing impressive output with tiny teams are aspirational references
social_job.internal_narrative: 'I shipped a full product this sprint and barely wrote any boilerplate code myself'
social_job.external_narrative: Posts a SAGE workflow demo that generates traction in the Indie Hackers / Hacker News community, signals technical sophistication to early investors

trigger_events: Just shipped v1, now drowning in support tickets, feature requests, and tech debt simultaneously
Lost a week re-explaining project context after hitting Claude/GPT context window limits
Tried to hire a contractor but onboarding took longer than doing the work themselves
Competitor with the same team size is shipping 2x faster (visible via public changelogs)
First B2B customer asking for audit log or change history before signing a contract
hiring_criteria.switch_threshold: Will adopt SAGE when: (1) context loss from AI session resets costs 4+ hours per week, OR (2) a specific domain workflow (firmware, ML pipeline, PCB) requires toolchain integration beyond raw LLM prompting
must_have_before_hiring: Zero-friction local setup — single 'make venv' plus 'make run' command
Works with free LLM providers (Gemini CLI, Ollama) — no surprise API bills on day one
Onboarding wizard generates working YAML config from plain English in under 5 minutes
Agent memory persists across sessions — no context amnesia
Can run entirely offline for IP-sensitive projects

dealbreakers: Setup complexity exceeds 30 minutes for a net-new solution
Agents require constant hand-holding — approval gate interrupts for every trivial action
No visibility into what agents are doing — black box execution
Memory does not compound — same mistakes made in session 10 as session 1
Cannot extend with custom MCP tools without touching framework Python
firing_criteria.tolerance_level: Low tolerance for friction and setup complexity; high tolerance for rough edges on features they do not use yet

current_solutions_displaced.why_insufficient: No persistent memory, no multi-agent coordination, no domain-specific toolchain, context resets destroy compounding value
Scoped to code only — no product strategy, documentation, UX, or GTM agents; no vector memory; no approval workflow
Purely manual — requires the founder to decompose every task; no AI-assisted planning or autonomous task execution
No agentic execution — generates text but does not act; no integration with code or deployment workflows
Rule-based only, no reasoning, cannot handle novel situations or produce creative output

tam.description: Global solo and micro-team technical founders and indie hackers actively building products
tam.source: GitHub 2024 Developer Survey (100M developers); Stack Overflow 2024 Survey; Indie Hackers self-reported founder count

sam.description: Technical founders with at least 1 live product and active AI tooling spend; English-language primary market
sam.rationale: Approximately 11% of GitHub developers self-identify as indie product builders with paying customers; 50% are English-primary

som_3yr.description: Reachable via open-source GitHub presence, Hacker News, Reddit, developer newsletters, ProductHunt — 5% SAM capture
som_3yr.growth_driver: Explosive growth in AI-native founder archetype post-GPT-4; SAGE's open-source model is the primary acquisition channel

willingness_to_pay_monthly_usd.rationale: Benchmarked against Cursor Pro ($20/mo), Linear ($8/user/mo), Notion AI ($10/user/mo) — total AI tooling spend typically $50–150/mo; SAGE replaces most of that stack

rice.note: Highest RICE score — enormous reach, self-serve acquisition, low effort because onboarding wizard and starter template already exist

success_metrics: Features shipped per week increases by 3x within 90 days of adopting SAGE (tracked via git commit frequency)
Context-switching time between AI tools drops below 30 minutes per day (self-reported in weekly check-in prompt)
Agent memory reuse rate above 60% — majority of new tasks leverage prior vector store context rather than re-prompting from scratch
Time-to-first-working-agent-for-new-domain under 10 minutes (measured via onboarding wizard telemetry)
LLM API cost per shipped feature decreases by 40% within 60 days due to provider switching and context reuse

pain_points.pain: Context amnesia — every new AI session starts from zero; the founder must re-explain the product, the codebase, the decisions made, and the constraints every single time, consuming 1–2 hours per day of pure overhead
pain_points.severity: Critical — directly kills the compounding value that makes AI tools worthwhile for a solo operator

pain_points.pain: Tool fragmentation — using ChatGPT for planning, Cursor for code, Notion AI for docs, Zapier for automation, and manually connecting the outputs; each tool has no awareness of the others
pain_points.severity: High — constant context-switching is the #1 source of cognitive load and lost momentum

pain_points.pain: Single point of failure — the founder is the only person who understands the full system; agents help generate output but cannot autonomously handle novel situations, meaning the founder is always on-call
pain_points.severity: Medium-High — unsustainable as the product grows; the founder cannot take a vacation without the product stalling

personas.name: David Okafor
personas.title: Director of AI Platform Engineering
personas.tagline: I need to give 200 teams a controlled on-ramp to agentic AI without letting any one team blow up the whole company.

context.industry: Financial services, enterprise SaaS, logistics, retail, healthcare IT
context.team_size: Platform team of 5–20 serving 50–500 internal developer teams
context.ai_maturity: Strategic evaluator — has run 3–5 AI pilots; now tasked with platformising the winners and decommissioning shadow-IT LLM experiments across business units

functional_job.statement: When centralising AI agent capabilities across dozens of product teams, I want a platform that provides consistent governance, cost visibility, and solution isolation so that individual teams can move fast without creating compliance or security liability at the company level.
sub_jobs: Provide each business unit a fully isolated agent environment (data, audit log, vector store) from a single platform deployment
Enforce LLM provider policy centrally (approved models, spend caps) while giving teams flexibility on prompts and tasks
Expose agent capabilities as an internal developer platform with self-service onboarding — wizard to working environment in under 1 day
Aggregate cost and usage metrics across all tenants for FinOps visibility
Integrate with existing identity (SSO/OIDC), ticketing (Jira), and secrets management (Vault)
functional_job.outcome_metric: Reduce time-to-first-agent-in-production per new team from 6 weeks to under 3 days; full cost allocation across business units by Q3

emotional_job.statement: I want to feel like the platform I am building is ahead of the business's appetite — not constantly reacting to teams that have already made a mess — and that when the inevitable security review comes, I can answer every question.
emotional_job.core_fear: A business unit deploys an LLM agent that exfiltrates customer PII, triggers a SOC 2 finding, or hallucinates financial data in a client-facing report — and it traces back to my platform
emotional_job.desired_feeling: Confident — the guardrails are architectural, not dependent on individual team compliance
emotional_job.tension: Under pressure from CTO to move fast and enable teams; under simultaneous pressure from CISO and Legal to ensure control and auditability

social_job.statement: I want to be seen by the CTO as the architect who enabled the company's AI transformation, and by the CISO as the person who made sure it did not become a liability.
social_job.peer_signal: Other platform architects at competing companies are presenting at re:Invent and KubeCon about their internal AI platforms; this persona wants to be on that stage
social_job.internal_narrative: 'Our AI platform serves 200 teams; every agent action is audited; we have had zero security incidents'
social_job.external_narrative: Publishes case study or conference talk on SAGE-based internal AI developer platform, establishing thought leadership in enterprise AI governance

trigger_events: CISO or Legal issued a moratorium on new LLM tool adoption pending a governance framework
FinOps team flagged uncontrolled AI API spend across business units with no cost allocation
CTO mandated 'platformise AI' as a Q1 OKR; platform team has 90 days to deliver
SOC 2 audit found that no audit trail exists for AI-assisted decisions in customer-facing systems
Three or more business units have built incompatible in-house agent frameworks; consolidation is required
hiring_criteria.switch_threshold: Will evaluate SAGE when: (1) shadow AI proliferation reaches 5+ independent business unit deployments, OR (2) first compliance finding attributable to uncontrolled AI usage is issued
must_have_before_hiring: Multi-tenant isolation is architectural — solution A's data cannot reach solution B's agents under any conditions
SAGE_SOLUTIONS_DIR pattern allows private solutions without modifying the framework repo
LLM provider switching at runtime without redeployment for model policy enforcement
REST API is the only public interface — no direct database access from business units
Horizontal scaling path exists — queue-based, stateless API layer

dealbreakers: Tenant data isolation is logical-only (same DB table with tenant_id filter) rather than physical (.sage/ per solution with separate SQLite and ChromaDB)
Framework requires production access to framework source repo to add a new solution
No cost metering or LLM call budgets per tenant or agent
Cannot integrate with corporate SSO/OIDC for the approval workflow
Vendor requires SaaS model — enterprise security policy mandates on-premise or private cloud deployment
Single point of failure on LLM provider — must support provider redundancy and failover
firing_criteria.tolerance_level: Very low tolerance for security and isolation gaps; moderate tolerance for UI polish and developer ergonomics gaps if API layer is solid

current_solutions_displaced.use_case: Company-wide LLM access with SSO and data isolation
current_solutions_displaced.why_insufficient: No agentic execution, no domain-specific runners, no per-team solution isolation, no customisable approval gates, no MCP tool integration

current_solutions_displaced.tool: Microsoft Azure AI Studio / OpenAI on Azure
current_solutions_displaced.use_case: Managed LLM APIs with enterprise security
current_solutions_displaced.why_insufficient: Requires significant custom engineering for agent orchestration, multi-tenant isolation, and HITL gates; no prebuilt domain runners; high implementation overhead

current_solutions_displaced.use_case: Internal developer portal for service discovery and scaffolding
current_solutions_displaced.why_insufficient: Not AI-native — no agent reasoning, no LLM integration, no proposal-and-approve workflow; only a catalog and template system
Observability only — no agent orchestration, no HITL gates, no domain-specific runners, no multi-tenant solution isolation

current_solutions_displaced.use_case: Custom per-team LLM wrappers and automation
current_solutions_displaced.why_insufficient: No governance, no audit trail, inconsistent quality, incompatible across teams, no compounding memory — the definition of shadow AI

tam.description: Global senior architects and directors responsible for internal AI/ML platform build-out at enterprises with 1,000+ employees
tam.source: Gartner 2025 AI Infrastructure Spending Forecast; LinkedIn Talent Insights — 'AI Platform Architect' title grew 310% YoY 2023–2025

sam.description: Platform architects at enterprises with active internal AI developer platform initiative and approved vendor evaluation budget
sam.rationale: Gartner estimates 20% of enterprises with 1,000+ employees initiated internal AI platform RFPs in 2025; of those, 100% have a named architect owner

som_3yr.description: Reachable via enterprise open-source adoption (SAGE deployed internally then formalised), direct outreach, and partner channel (SIs, GSIs) — 1.5% SAM capture
som_3yr.growth_driver: Enterprise AI platformisation wave driven by SOC 2 and ISO 27001 audit requirements for LLM governance; SAGE's audit-first architecture is a direct response

willingness_to_pay_monthly_usd.rationale: Enterprise contracts priced per number of active solutions/tenants; benchmarked against Datadog ($2,500/mo for equivalent observability scope) and GitHub Enterprise ($21/user/mo for 500 developers = $10,500/mo)

rice.note: Lower RICE due to small reachable cohort and high sales cycle effort; but highest ACV per deal — enterprise contract value 5–10x Persona 1. Long-term strategic moat.

success_metrics: Number of internal teams onboarded to SAGE platform per quarter — target: 10 teams per quarter by end of year 1
Shadow AI incidents eliminated — reduction in security and compliance findings attributable to uncontrolled LLM usage from baseline to zero within 6 months
Time-to-first-agent-in-production per new team reduced from 6 weeks to under 3 days (measured from onboarding wizard initiation to first approved proposal)
LLM spend visibility — 100% of AI API costs attributed to specific business unit and solution by end of Q3
Platform NPS from internal developer teams above 40 within 12 months of launch (measured via quarterly internal survey)

pain_points.pain: Shadow AI proliferation — individual business units have deployed 5–15 incompatible LLM integrations with no audit trail, no governance, no isolation, and no visibility to the platform team; the CISO is aware and demanding action within 90 days
pain_points.severity: Critical — direct compliance and security liability; a single incident could result in regulatory action or customer data breach

pain_points.pain: No enterprise-grade agentic AI platform exists off the shelf — every available option (Azure AI Studio, LangSmith, Vertex AI) requires 6–12 months of custom engineering to add multi-tenant isolation, HITL gates, domain runners, and cost metering
pain_points.severity: High — forces the platform team to build from scratch, consuming headcount that could be delivering product value

pain_points.pain: CTO versus CISO tension is unresolvable without architecture — CTO demands AI-enabled teams by Q1; CISO demands audit trail and isolation before any AI tool is approved; without a platform that satisfies both, the platform architect is caught in the middle indefinitely
pain_points.severity: High — political and organisational, not just technical; blocking the platform team's ability to make decisions and move forward

shared_functional_jobs: Compound agent knowledge over time (vector memory) — all 3 personas need context that does not reset
Human approval gate for high-stakes actions — all 3 personas need control, for different reasons (compliance, quality, governance)
Multi-LLM provider flexibility — cost, policy, and capability reasons differ but the requirement is the same across all three

approval_gate_philosophy.P1_regulated: Mandatory for all agent proposals — compliance requirement, non-negotiable
approval_gate_philosophy.P2_indie: Optional and configurable — interrupts for routine tasks are a dealbreaker
approval_gate_philosophy.P3_enterprise: Mandatory for cross-team and customer-facing actions; optional for internal dev tasks

deployment_model.P1_regulated: On-premise or private cloud; data residency in regulatory jurisdiction
deployment_model.P2_indie: Local or personal cloud; zero infrastructure overhead
deployment_model.P3_enterprise: Private cloud with SSO, network policies, and FinOps integration

primary_value_metric.P1_regulated: Audit coverage percentage and compliance documentation velocity
primary_value_metric.P2_indie: Features shipped per week and context-switching time saved
primary_value_metric.P3_enterprise: Number of teams onboarded per quarter and shadow-AI incidents eliminated

persona_priority_for_sage_roadmap.immediate_focus: P2 (Indie Founder) — highest RICE at 159,375; self-serve acquisition; open-source flywheel already in motion
persona_priority_for_sage_roadmap.strategic_moat: P1 (Regulated Lead) — highest willingness-to-pay; strongest differentiation via audit trail and HITL gate; defensible against generic LLM tools
persona_priority_for_sage_roadmap.enterprise_upside: P3 (Enterprise Architect) — lowest near-term RICE but highest ACV per deal; multi-tenant isolation already architecturally present; unlocks a fundamentally different revenue tier
